test_cases.test_case: Successful login with valid credentials redirects to Dashboard for teacher
preconditions: User is not authenticated (logged out) and has access to the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: The Dashboard page is displayed; the top navigation bar and course navigation are visible, showing the user has been authenticated and landed on the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Access as a guest redirects to Dashboard in guest mode
preconditions: User is not authenticated (logged out) and has access to the Login page
steps: 1. Click the 'Access as a guest' button
test_cases.expected_result: The Dashboard page is displayed; the top navigation bar is visible and the UI shows an indicator that the session is unauthenticated guest access

test_cases.test_case: Open Cookies notice displays cookie usage information
preconditions: User is not authenticated (logged out) and has access to the Login page
steps: 1. Click the 'Cookies notice' button
test_cases.expected_result: A Cookies information dialog/panel opens displaying cookie usage information

test_cases.test_case: Disabled 'Lost password?' link is visible but not clickable on test site
preconditions: User is not authenticated (logged out) and has access to the Login page
The 'Lost password?' link is disabled on this test site
steps: 1. Attempt to click the 'Lost password?' link
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Lost password?' link remains visually disabled and no navigation or dialog is opened

test_cases.test_case: Leave Username blank and submit (required text field representative)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is logged out
steps: 1. Ensure the User_Name field is empty (leave it blank)
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error message "Invalid or empty credentials" is displayed; the form is not submitted; Password field is cleared; User_Name remains blank (no value retained); the user stays on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Leave Password blank and submit (password field representative)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is logged out
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the User_Name field
2. Leave the Password field empty
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error message "Invalid or empty credentials" is displayed; the form is not submitted; Password field is cleared (remains empty); User_Name value is retained on the form; the user stays on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Submit with all required fields empty
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is logged out
steps: 1. Ensure the User_Name field is empty
2. Ensure the Password field is empty
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error message "Invalid or empty credentials" is displayed; the form is not submitted; Password field is cleared (remains empty); User_Name remains blank; the user stays on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Submit invalid credentials (authentication failure)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is logged out
steps: 1. Enter <existing username> in the User_Name field
2. Enter <incorrect password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error message "Invalid or empty credentials" is displayed; the form is not submitted; Password field is cleared; User_Name remains populated with the entered username; the user stays on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Lost password?' link when it is disabled on this site
preconditions: User is on the Login page
Test site has the Lost password? link disabled
steps: 1. Observe the 'Lost password?' link on the page
2. Attempt to click the 'Lost password?' link
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Lost password?' link is displayed as disabled or non-interactive and is not clickable; clicking it has no effect and there is no navigation to a password-recovery page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Access as a guest' while already authenticated (precondition failure)
preconditions: User is authenticated (logged in) and on the Dashboard
steps: 1. While authenticated as <valid user>, navigate to the Login page
2. Locate the 'Access as a guest' button
3. Attempt to click the 'Access as a guest' button
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Access as a guest' action is not allowed when the user is authenticated: the button is either not visible or is disabled/non-interactive; clicking it does not start an unauthenticated session and no navigation to a guest Dashboard occurs; user remains authenticated

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to use Log in action while already authenticated (precondition failure)
preconditions: User is authenticated (logged in) and on the Dashboard
steps: 1. While authenticated as <valid user>, navigate to the Login page
2. Attempt to interact with the 'Log in' button or submit the login form
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Log in' action is not allowed when the user is already authenticated: the Log in button is either not visible or is disabled/non-interactive; attempting to submit does not create a new login session or switch to an unauthenticated/guest session; user remains authenticated

test_cases.test_case: Very long username is preserved after failed login
preconditions: User is logged out and on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) in the User_Name field
2. Enter <an incorrect password> in the Password field
3. Click Log in
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error is shown; Password field is cleared; User_Name input retains the very long string exactly as entered (visible in the username input) — the login attempt is blocked with the inline error

test_cases.test_case: Username with emoji and non-Latin Unicode characters is retained on failure
preconditions: User is logged out and on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter a string containing emoji and non-Latin Unicode characters in the User_Name field
2. Enter <an incorrect password> in the Password field
3. Click Log in
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error is shown; Password field is cleared; User_Name input retains the exact Unicode/emoji characters as entered (visible in the username input) — the login attempt is blocked with the inline error

test_cases.test_case: Password cleared and username retained when credentials are invalid
preconditions: User is logged out and on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter any value in the User_Name field
2. Enter any incorrect value in the Password field
3. Click Log in
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error displays the configured message "Invalid or empty credentials"; Password field is cleared; User_Name field remains populated with the value entered (the login attempt is blocked with the inline error)

test_cases.test_case: Disabled 'Lost password?' link is non-interactive
preconditions: User is logged out and on the Login page
steps: 1. Click the 'Lost password?' link
test_cases.expected_result: No navigation or modal opens; the 'Lost password?' link remains on the page and the login form remains visible (click produces no effect)

test_cases.test_case: Open New Event creation interface from Calendar block
preconditions: Authenticated user session (logged in)
User is enrolled in one or more courses
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' item in the top navigation to open the Dashboard
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'New event' button
test_cases.expected_result: A New Event creation interface is displayed (dialog or panel) allowing entry of event details; the interface is visible on top of the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Navigate calendar to the previous month
preconditions: Authenticated user session (logged in)
Dashboard is accessible
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' item in the top navigation to open the Dashboard
2. Note the current month/year shown in the Calendar block heading
3. Click the 'Previous Month' button (left arrow) in the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar view updates to show the previous month; the month/year heading in the Calendar block reflects the previous month

test_cases.test_case: Navigate calendar to the next month
preconditions: Authenticated user session (logged in)
Dashboard is accessible
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' item in the top navigation to open the Dashboard
2. Note the current month/year shown in the Calendar block heading
3. Click the 'Next Month' button (right arrow) in the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar view updates to show the next month; the month/year heading in the Calendar block reflects the next month

test_cases.test_case: Open the Full calendar view via link
preconditions: Authenticated user session (logged in)
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' item in the top navigation to open the Dashboard
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Full calendar' link
test_cases.expected_result: The Full calendar view opens (navigation to the dedicated calendar page); the page displays a calendar heading with month/year and a calendar grid

test_cases.test_case: Open the Import or export calendars page via link
preconditions: Authenticated user session (logged in)
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' item in the top navigation to open the Dashboard
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Import or export calendars' link
test_cases.expected_result: The calendar import/export page opens (navigation to calendar data management); the page displays import/export controls for calendar data

test_cases.test_case: Use Timeline search to filter activities by name or type
preconditions: Authenticated user session (logged in)
Dashboard is accessible
Timeline contains multiple upcoming activities across enrolled courses
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' item in the top navigation to open the Dashboard
2. In the Timeline block, enter <search term matching an activity name or type> in the Search field and press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline displays only activities matching <search term>; unrelated activities are no longer visible in the Timeline

test_cases.test_case: Timeline shows empty state when no items exist in the selected time range
preconditions: Authenticated user session (logged in)
Dashboard is accessible
User is enrolled in courses but has no activities in the target time range
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' item in the top navigation to open the Dashboard
2. In the Timeline block, open the Time Range dropdown and select <time range with no activities>
3. Observe the Timeline content area
test_cases.expected_result: An empty state is displayed in the Timeline indicating there are no upcoming activities in the selected time range

test_cases.test_case: Filter calendar by a single course using 'All courses' dropdown
preconditions: Authenticated user session (logged in)
Dashboard is accessible
User has calendar events across multiple courses (e.g., <course A> and <course B>)
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' item in the top navigation to open the Dashboard
2. In the Calendar block, open the 'All courses' dropdown and select <specific course>
3. Observe the calendar month view
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar displays only events belonging to <specific course>; dates that have events show the event names inline and events from other courses are no longer visible in the calendar grid

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access Dashboard page
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no active session)
steps: 1. Ensure the user is logged out (clear any session/cookies).
2. Navigate to the Dashboard URL or click 'Dashboard' in the top navigation.
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked: the Login page or sign-in prompt is shown; Dashboard content (personalized greeting, Timeline block and Calendar block) is not visible and the user is not taken to the Dashboard.

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot open New event creation
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no active session)
steps: 1. Ensure the user is logged out.
2. Navigate to the Dashboard URL.
3. Click the 'New event' button in the Calendar block.
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked due to missing authentication: the New event creation interface does not open; the user is redirected to the login page or a sign-in prompt is displayed; no new-event form appears on the Dashboard.

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot open Full calendar view
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no active session)
steps: 1. Ensure the user is logged out.
2. Navigate to the Dashboard URL.
3. Click the 'Full calendar' link at the bottom of the Calendar block.
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked due to missing authentication: the Full calendar view does not open; the user is redirected to the login page or a sign-in prompt is displayed; the Dashboard remains displayed and no navigation to the full calendar occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot open Import or export calendars
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no active session)
steps: 1. Ensure the user is logged out.
2. Navigate to the Dashboard URL.
3. Click the 'Import or export calendars' link at the bottom of the Calendar block.
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked due to missing authentication: the calendar import/export page or UI does not open; the user is redirected to the login page or a sign-in prompt is displayed; no import/export interface is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Search with very long input returns empty timeline
preconditions: Authenticated user session (logged in)
Dashboard main view is open
At least one timeline item exists for the user (to differentiate from a global empty dashboard)
steps: 1. Focus the Timeline Search field
2. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) into the Search field
3. Press Enter or click the Search icon
test_cases.expected_result: Search executes successfully; Timeline displays the empty-state panel with no items visible (no matching activities shown)

test_cases.test_case: Search with leading/trailing whitespace matches existing item after trim
preconditions: Authenticated user session (logged in)
Dashboard main view is open
There is a known timeline item with a visible name
steps: 1. Focus the Timeline Search field
2. Enter the exact name of an existing timeline item with leading and trailing spaces around it (e.g., prepend and append spaces)
3. Press Enter or click the Search icon
test_cases.expected_result: Search input is trimmed; timeline search succeeds and displays the matching item(s) (the matching item appears in the Timeline with its normal name, no leading/trailing spaces displayed)

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click New event does not open duplicate creation interfaces
preconditions: Authenticated user session (logged in)
Dashboard main view is open
steps: 1. Click the Calendar block's New event button
2. Immediately click the New event button again (second click within a short interval, e.g., 500ms)
test_cases.expected_result: Second click is blocked; only one New event creation interface is displayed (no duplicate modal/page instances appear)

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to previous month removes current-date highlight when crossing month boundary
preconditions: Authenticated user session (logged in)
Dashboard main view is open
Calendar block initially displays the current month with the current date highlighted
steps: 1. Click the Calendar block's Previous Month button once
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to the previous month succeeds; calendar month/year heading updates to the previous month and the current date highlight is no longer shown in the displayed month view

test_cases.test_case: Reset page to default while Edit mode is on
preconditions: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)
User has a customized Dashboard layout (non-default blocks or positions)
steps: 1. Check the Edit Mode checkbox in the Dashboard Edit Mode control to enable Edit mode
2. Click the 'Reset page to default' button in the action bar
test_cases.expected_result: The Dashboard displays the default set of blocks in their default positions; any custom blocks or custom positions that were present before are no longer visible on the page

test_cases.test_case: Open the Add a block page from Edit mode
preconditions: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)
steps: 1. Check the Edit Mode checkbox in the Dashboard Edit Mode control to enable Edit mode
2. Click the '+ Add a block' button below the Dashboard heading
test_cases.expected_result: The Add a block page opens and lists available block types: Comments, Course overview, Latest announcements, Latest badges, Learning plans, Logged in user, Mentees, Online users, Private files, Random glossary entry, Recently accessed courses, Starred courses, Tags, Text, Upcoming events

test_cases.test_case: Cancel out of Add a block page and return to Dashboard
preconditions: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)
steps: 1. Check the Edit Mode checkbox in the Dashboard Edit Mode control to enable Edit mode
2. Click the '+ Add a block' button to open the Add a block page
3. Click the 'Cancel' link on the Add a block page
test_cases.expected_result: The Dashboard page is displayed; the Add a block page is closed and the Dashboard shows the same blocks and layout that were visible before opening Add a block

test_cases.test_case: Open block configuration UI from a block's options menu in Edit mode
preconditions: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)
Dashboard contains at least one <existing block>
steps: 1. Check the Edit Mode checkbox in the Dashboard Edit Mode control to enable Edit mode
2. For <existing block>, open its options menu (three-dot menu)
3. Click 'Configure' in the options menu
test_cases.expected_result: The block configuration UI is displayed for <existing block> (a configuration panel or modal with controls to edit the block is visible)

test_cases.test_case: Move a block to a new position while Edit mode is on
preconditions: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)
Dashboard contains at least two <existing blocks> so a move is possible
steps: 1. Check the Edit Mode checkbox in the Dashboard Edit Mode control to enable Edit mode
2. For <existing block>, open its options menu (three-dot menu) and click 'Move' (or use the block's Move handle)
3. Drag the <existing block>'s move handle to the target position/column and release to place it
test_cases.expected_result: The <existing block> is displayed in its new position on the Dashboard (its visual position/order has changed accordingly)

test_cases.test_case: Delete a block from the Dashboard in Edit mode
preconditions: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)
Dashboard contains at least one <existing block>
steps: 1. Check the Edit Mode checkbox in the Dashboard Edit Mode control to enable Edit mode
2. For <existing block>, open its options menu (three-dot menu)
3. Click 'Delete' in the options menu
test_cases.expected_result: The <existing block> is no longer visible on the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Edit mode reveals top action buttons and per-block edit controls
preconditions: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)
Dashboard contains at least one <existing block>
steps: 1. Check the Edit Mode checkbox in the Dashboard Edit Mode control to enable Edit mode
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Reset page to default' button is visible at the top right and the '+ Add a block' button is visible below the Dashboard heading; each block displays a move handle and a three-dot options menu

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access Edit mode controls
preconditions: User is not logged in
Dashboard page exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page as an unauthenticated user
2. Attempt to open Edit mode by clicking the Edit mode toggle or visiting the Edit mode URL
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page or shown a login prompt; Edit mode controls (Edit toggle, 'Reset page to default', '+ Add a block', move handles, options menu) are not available/visible on the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Reset page to default button is not available when Edit mode is off
preconditions: Logged in as a user with edit permission
Dashboard loaded with Edit mode OFF (Edit_Mode == false)
steps: 1. Ensure the Edit mode checkbox is unchecked (Edit mode OFF)
2. Attempt to click the top-right 'Reset page to default' button
test_cases.expected_result: 'Reset page to default' button is not visible or clickable while Edit mode is off; attempting to click it does not revert the user's dashboard layout and the dashboard remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: '+ Add a block' action is not available when Edit mode is off
preconditions: Logged in as a user with edit permission
Dashboard loaded with Edit mode OFF (Edit_Mode == false)
steps: 1. Ensure the Edit mode checkbox is unchecked (Edit mode OFF)
2. Attempt to click the '+ Add a block' button below the Dashboard heading
test_cases.expected_result: '+ Add a block' button is not visible or clickable while Edit mode is off; no Add a block page opens and the Dashboard remains displayed

test_cases.test_case: User without dashboard edit permission cannot open Add a block page (precondition failure)
preconditions: Logged in as <user without dashboard edit permission>
Dashboard loaded
steps: 1. Log in as <user without dashboard edit permission>
2. If Edit mode toggle is present, enable Edit mode (if not present, skip this step)
3. Click the '+ Add a block' button (if visible) or navigate to the Add a block page URL
test_cases.expected_result: The Add a block page does not open; either the '+ Add a block' control is not visible to this user or clicking it is blocked; the user remains on the Dashboard and no Add a block UI is shown (no block can be added)

test_cases.test_case: Configure action is blocked for users without edit permission
preconditions: Logged in as <user without dashboard edit permission>
Dashboard loaded with at least one block present
steps: 1. Log in as <user without dashboard edit permission>
2. Open the options menu for an existing block
3. Attempt to click the 'Configure' row action
test_cases.expected_result: 'Configure' is not present or not actionable for this user; clicking does not open the block configuration UI and the block remains unchanged; no configuration UI is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Move handle is not available and blocks cannot be moved when Edit mode is off
preconditions: Logged in as a user with edit permission
Dashboard loaded with Edit mode OFF (Edit_Mode == false)
At least one block is present
steps: 1. Ensure the Edit mode checkbox is unchecked (Edit mode OFF)
2. Attempt to grab the block's move handle and drag the block to a new position
test_cases.expected_result: Move handle is not visible when Edit mode is off and dragging is not possible; attempting to drag does not change block position and the dashboard layout remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Delete action is blocked for users without edit permission
preconditions: Logged in as <user without dashboard edit permission>
Dashboard loaded with at least one block present
steps: 1. Log in as <user without dashboard edit permission>
2. Open the options menu for an existing block
3. Attempt to click the 'Delete' row action
test_cases.expected_result: 'Delete' is not present or not actionable for this user; clicking does not remove the block and no deletion confirmation or change occurs; the block remains on the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Reset page to default' when layout is already default
preconditions: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)
User's dashboard currently matches the system default layout
steps: 1. Toggle the Edit Mode checkbox to ON
2. Click the 'Reset page to default' action in the action bar
test_cases.expected_result: Reset action succeeds: the Dashboard remains in the default layout (no visible layout change) and no error is shown; Edit Mode remains active and the UI shows no indication of failure

test_cases.test_case: Invoke Reset immediately after moving a block (persistence-timing edge)
preconditions: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)
Dashboard contains at least one block in a known position
steps: 1. Toggle the Edit Mode checkbox to ON
2. Use the move handle on a block to drag it to a different position on the page (perform the drag action)
3. Immediately after completing the drag, click the 'Reset page to default' action (do not wait for any additional UI persistence confirmation)
test_cases.expected_result: Reset action succeeds: the Dashboard visibly reverts to the default layout (the moved block returns to its original default position) and no error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Delete all blocks until the repeating group is empty
preconditions: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)
Dashboard contains one or more blocks
steps: 1. Toggle the Edit Mode checkbox to ON
2. For the first visible block, open its Options menu (three-dot menu)
3. Click 'Delete' in the Options menu for that block
4. Repeat steps 2–3 until no blocks remain in the Dashboard repeating group
5. Toggle the Edit Mode checkbox to OFF
test_cases.expected_result: Deletion succeeds: the Dashboard displays no blocks (an empty-dashboard state is visible) and no error is shown; the removal of all blocks is persisted for the user

test_cases.test_case: Open a block's Options menu then toggle Edit Mode off
preconditions: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)
At least one block is present on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Toggle the Edit Mode checkbox to ON
2. Click the Options menu (three-dot) for any existing block to open it
3. Toggle the Edit Mode checkbox to OFF while the Options menu is open
test_cases.expected_result: Toggling Edit Mode off succeeds: the Options menu and move handles are no longer visible and the Options menu closes immediately; the Dashboard returns to view mode without an error

test_cases.test_case: Open a course by clicking the course name link
preconditions: User logged in as <Teacher>
Authenticated user (teacher) with access to one or more courses.
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page via the top navigation
2. Locate the course card for <target course>
3. Click the Course Name link on the <target course> card
test_cases.expected_result: The Course main page for <target course> opens; the page heading displays the course title and the course main header is visible

test_cases.test_case: Star a course from the card menu and confirm it is pinned to the top
preconditions: User logged in as <Teacher>
Authenticated user (teacher) with access to one or more courses.
The My Courses page is open showing multiple course cards including <target course>
steps: 1. Ensure the My Courses page is visible (navigate there if not)
2. Open the three-dot menu on the card for <target course>
3. Click 'Star this course' in the card menu
test_cases.expected_result: The course card for <target course> is displayed as the first item at the top of the Course Cards grid (pinned position)

test_cases.test_case: Remove a course from view via the card menu and confirm it is hidden
preconditions: User logged in as <Teacher>
Authenticated user (teacher) with access to one or more courses.
The My Courses page is open showing the course card for <target course>
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page if not already open
2. Open the three-dot menu on the card for <target course>
3. Click 'Remove from view' in the card menu
test_cases.expected_result: The course card for <target course> is no longer visible in the Course Cards grid; other course cards remain visible

test_cases.test_case: Filter courses by Status = 'Starred' and verify only starred courses are shown
preconditions: User logged in as <Teacher>
Authenticated user (teacher) with access to one or more courses.
At least one course is already starred in the My Courses list
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Open the Status dropdown
3. Select 'Starred' from the Status dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Course Cards grid displays only starred courses; non-starred course cards are not visible

test_cases.test_case: Search for a course using the Search field and verify results are filtered
preconditions: User logged in as <Teacher>
Authenticated user (teacher) with access to one or more courses.
The My Courses page is open with multiple course cards
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Enter <search term> in the Search field and submit the search
test_cases.expected_result: Course Cards grid displays only cards matching <search term>; unrelated course cards are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Change Layout to 'List' and verify the courses are shown in list layout
preconditions: User logged in as <Teacher>
Authenticated user (teacher) with access to one or more courses.
The My Courses page is open
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page
2. Open the Layout dropdown
3. Select 'List' from the Layout dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: The courses area switches to List layout, displaying each course as a horizontal row with course name and category visible

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access My Courses page
preconditions: User is not authenticated
steps: 1. As an unauthenticated user, navigate to the My Courses page URL
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked: user is redirected to the authentication / login page; the My Courses Course Cards grid is not rendered or accessible on the screen

test_cases.test_case: Non-teacher role does not see My Courses navigation or course grid
preconditions: Authenticated user with role <non-teacher role> (e.g., Student)
steps: 1. Sign in as an authenticated user with role <non-teacher role>
2. Observe the top navigation bar for the My Courses link
3. Attempt to navigate to the My Courses page by clicking the top navigation or by entering the My Courses URL directly
test_cases.expected_result: Top navigation does not show a My Courses link for this user role; if the URL is opened directly, the Course Cards grid is not rendered and row-actions (Star, Remove from view) are not available — the UI displays an access-restricted / no-access state instead of course content

test_cases.test_case: Authenticated teacher with no course access is blocked from opening a course
preconditions: Authenticated user with role <teacher> who has zero courses (does not meet 'access to one or more courses' precondition)
steps: 1. Sign in as an authenticated teacher who has no course enrollments or access
2. Navigate to the My Courses page
test_cases.expected_result: Page does not render any Course Cards (empty-state shown); there is no course name link to open a course and therefore the 'Open Course' action cannot be performed; Star and Remove row-actions are not present

test_cases.test_case: Row action 'Star this course' is blocked for users who do not meet page precondition
preconditions: Authenticated user with role <non-teacher role> or user that does not meet page precondition
steps: 1. Sign in as an authenticated user with role <non-teacher role>
2. Navigate directly to the My Courses page URL
3. If any course card is visible, open the card's three-dot menu
4. Click the 'Star this course' row-action
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Star this course' action does not complete: no pinning/reordering occurs and the course is not moved to the top; the row-action is either not selectable or an inline access/error indicator is shown next to the action; Course Cards order remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Very long search string (200+ chars) is entered
preconditions: Authenticated user (teacher) with access to one or more courses
My Courses page is open and the Search field is visible
steps: 1. Focus the Search field
2. Type a very long string of 200+ characters into the Search field
3. Press Enter (or click the Search icon) to run the search

test_cases.test_case: Navigate between all top-level course tabs and return to Course tab

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open an activity from a collapsed section (visible_when violation)
preconditions: User is logged in and has opened the course (course loaded on Course tab)

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open an activity when its section has been collapsed via Collapse All

test_cases.test_case: After using Collapse All, expand a single section via its Toggle_Section and verify only that section expands
preconditions: User is logged in and viewing a course with multiple sections, at least one section contains at least one activity
steps: 1. Click the Collapse All link
2. Click the Toggle_Section button for a single section (Section A)

test_cases.test_case: Click Collapse All twice in succession to verify idempotent behavior

steps: 1. Click the Collapse All link
2. Click the Collapse All link a second time
test_cases.expected_result: Second Collapse All click succeeds with no error shown; all sections remain collapsed (Is_Collapsed == true) and there is no visible error or change beyond the collapsed state

test_cases.test_case: Expand a section via Toggle_Section and open an activity to verify activity links and icons are visible and navigable
preconditions: User is logged in and viewing a course with at least one section containing at least one activity
steps: 1. If the target section is collapsed, click its Toggle_Section button to expand it
2. Confirm the section's Activities list is visible
3. Click the Activity_Name link for an activity in the now-visible Activities list
test_cases.expected_result: Toggle_Section succeeds; the Activities list for the section is visible showing activity type icons and Activity_Name links; clicking Activity_Name succeeds and the UI navigates to the activity/resource page

module_context.summary: Provides the authentication form allowing users to enter credentials or access the site as a guest and shows inline errors for invalid submissions.
module_context.where_it_fits: The Login module is the entry point to the application and precedes access to the top navigation and course/dashboard areas; successful authentication redirects the user to the Dashboard where the top navigation bar and subsequent course navigation become available.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is not authenticated (logged out) and has access to the login page

module_context.summary: The Dashboard provides a personalized landing page that displays a Timeline of upcoming teaching actions and a Calendar view of events across the user’s courses.
module_context.where_it_fits: The Dashboard is reachable from the top navigation bar (Home, Dashboard, My Courses) and serves as the user's personalized landing area after login; it aggregates course activity and links into the dedicated calendar view and calendar import/export workflows. Within the course lifecycle it sits at the global level (outside individual Course pages) as an overview and navigation hub to course-specific activity and the full calendar.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user session (logged in); user account exists and may be enrolled in one or more courses to populate Timeline and Calendar.

module_context.summary: A mode for the Dashboard that enables adding, configuring, moving, and deleting page blocks with layout changes persisted per user and an option to reset the page to default.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is reached from the top navigation via the Dashboard link and activates while viewing the Dashboard page; it is part of the user-facing customization workflow that lets users personalize their Dashboard layout within the application's main navigation. It complements other course- and activity-level editing areas but specifically controls the Dashboard's block layout and content.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in user on the Dashboard page with permission to edit the Dashboard (Edit mode available)

module_context.summary: Displays all courses a teacher has access to as visual cards with filtering, searching, sorting, layout options, and per-course actions (star or remove from view).
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation via My Courses (alongside Home and Dashboard), this page provides an index of the user's courses and serves as the entry point before navigating into a specific Course Page via the course name link.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user (teacher) with access to one or more courses.

module_context.summary: Displays a selected course’s full content organized into collapsible sections with activities and resources listed and a course-level tab bar.
module_context.where_it_fits: Reached from the top navigation (e.g., My Courses or Dashboard) when a user opens a specific course, this is the primary course view located beneath the course title and horizontal tab bar (Course, Participants, Grades, Activities, Competencies) and works with the Course Index sidebar to navigate sections and activities. It is the main lifecycle screen for viewing course materials and activity links.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has opened a specific course (the course exists and the user has permission to view it).

module_context.module_title: Course Edit Mode and Activity Chooser
module_context.summary: Provides an authoring interface on the Course page that exposes inline editing controls for sections and activities and a modal Activity Chooser for adding new activities or resources.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is part of the Course Page experience: when a user viewing a course (via Home > My Courses or Dashboard > My Courses) enables Edit mode, the course page switches to this authoring UI and the Activity Chooser modal is launched from the "+ Add an activity or resource" control within course sections.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in, viewing a specific Course page, and has instructor/teacher permissions (ability to edit the course).

module_context.summary: A multi-panel form for configuring all settings of a course assignment including availability, submission and feedback types, grading, access restrictions, completion, and notifications.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessed from the Activity Chooser while in a course (typically via Course Edit Mode) and used during course authoring; upon save it creates the assignment and returns to or opens the assignment page within the Course Page where it appears in the Course Index and activity breadcrumbs. It is part of the course content creation flow reachable from the course interface (Course tab, Course Index sidebar, and edit controls).
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is in a specific Course with editing enabled and has permission to add activities (e.g., logged in as a Teacher) and the Activity Chooser has been opened.

module_context.summary: Provides a collapsible form for configuring course metadata, appearance, format, enrollment and completion settings, and media with inline validation and save/cancel actions.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessed from a course's horizontal tab bar (the Settings tab shown to teachers) when viewing a course; saving returns to the course page while canceling leaves settings unchanged. It sits alongside other course-level tools such as Participants, Grades, and Activities and is part of the course configuration lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in, viewing an existing course, and has teacher/editing permissions (Settings tab visible).

module_context.summary: Displays and manages the list of users enrolled in a specific course, providing filters, role editing, enrollment dialog, per-user actions, and bulk operations.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from within a course via the horizontal tab bar as the Participants tab (Course -> Participants) and sits alongside Course, Grades, and Activities in the course management workflow; it is used when managing enrollment and participant settings for a given course.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and viewing a specific course page; the course exists and the user has permissions to view/manage participants (e.g., teacher or manager role) if enrollment or role changes are required.

module_context.summary: Displays an assignment's metadata, attached files, read-only grading summary metrics, and navigation for grading, submissions, settings, and advanced grading from the teacher perspective.
module_context.where_it_fits: Shown when viewing an assignment activity within a course (accessible via the course page and Course Index and represented in breadcrumbs like Course → Assignment); it sits in the activity-level workflow where teachers review assignment details and launch grading or submission management.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a Teacher and viewing a course that contains the assignment (the assignment activity already exists).

module_context.summary: Displays a table of all student submission records for an assignment with search/filter controls, per-student actions to grade, and optional quick inline grading.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from within a course's activity view for an Assignment (breadcrumbs show the path from the course), and reachable via the Course tab and Course Index sidebar when an Assignment activity is selected. It is part of the grading workflow that teachers use after students submit work and complements the Gradebook and Assignment — Teacher View modules.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is viewing a course that contains the specific Assignment activity and is logged in with a role that has grading/teacher permissions; the assignment has at least one student submission (or is ready to receive submissions).

module_context.summary: A spreadsheet-style report showing enrolled students as rows and activities as columns with filtering, per-cell and per-column editing, inline save, and class averages.
module_context.where_it_fits: Reached from a course's horizontal tab bar under Grades (and also via the user menu's Grades link), the Grader report sits in the grading workflow as the teacher-facing interface for reviewing and editing grades after activities and submissions exist.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a teacher and viewing a course that has enrolled students and graded activities/submissions.

module_context.summary: Displays the teacher's profile information including name and initials, contact and privacy details, links to related course profiles and content, reports, and login activity.
module_context.where_it_fits: This page is reached from the top navigation's user menu (which contains links for Profile, Grades, Calendar, Private Files, Reports, and Preferences) and sits within the account-related area of the site alongside other personal pages; it complements course-level pages (e.g., Course Page and Participants) by providing the user's global information and links to their course profiles.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated session as a teacher (logged in)

module_context.summary: Allows a user to edit their personal profile information, upload or change their user picture, and modify optional/custom fields via a multi-section form with validation.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation user menu under Profile, this module is part of the user account management flow and is reached from the global site header; after successful updates it returns the user to their Profile page. It sits alongside other account-related screens like Profile, Profile Edit, and Preferences in the user settings lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in user who has opened their Profile page and has permission to edit their profile

module_context.summary: Ends the current authenticated session and redirects the user to the login page.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the user menu in the top navigation bar (right-side user initials dropdown) and used as the final step in the session lifecycle; after activation the app returns to the Login page and protected areas (Dashboard, Course pages, etc.) require re-authentication. It is a global-level action available from any page via the top navigation.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is currently authenticated (logged in) with access to the top navigation user menu.
